creator: stas@FreeBSD.org
text:
On Tue, 17 Jul 2007 09:52:43 -0700
Stephen Hurd <shurd@sasktel.net> mentioned:

> >>>   
> >>> +	${MKDIR} ${PREFIX}/share/applications/
> >>>   
> >>>
> >>> Why not use ${MKDIR} ${DESKTOPDIR}/ ?
> >>>
> >>>   
> >>>       
> >> Fixed!
> >>
> >>     
> >
> > Approved.
> >   
> 
> Hrm, that brings up an interesting question...
> If DESKTOPDIR is overridden, the pkg-plist is then wrong.  However, 
> since DESKTOPDIR isn't specified relative to PREFIX, should you use a 
> PLIST_SUB to put the absolute path in the pkg-plist?  Or is that "wrong" 
> too?
> 
> In the Makefile:
> PLIST_SUB= DESKTOPDIR=${DESKTOPDIR)
> 
> In the plist:
> %%DESKTOPDIR%%/syncterm.desktop
> @dirrmtry %%DESKTOPDIR%%
> 
> 
> 

I think the following will do the trick:
PLIST_SUB+=	DESKTOPDIR="${_DESKTOPDIR_REL}"

-- 
Stanislav Sedov
ST4096-RIPE
